files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index 24cc839..3c32782 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -182,2 +182,9 @@ Day 175 / Week 25 observations:
 - Support/retention is still the limiting factor: 409 cancellations, S2 issue age 1.19 days, and social posts ask for concrete tax-regression fixes plus higher caps.
 - Week 25 adjustment: defer tier-7 R&D until cash has buffer, increase support/dev, increase B/C quotas, and post a concrete Day 182 tax-regression fix target.
+
+Day 182 / Week 26 observations:
+- Cash rebuilt to $1.97M. Subscription payments were $2.10M against about $1.32M in operating costs, so tier-7 R&D is now affordable with buffer.
+- S2 content remains efficient at $5k/day: 4,763 paid content subscribers from 6,418 leads, and network referrals added 3,480 S2 subscribers.
+- Peak usage was 8.8M/day, well below tier-5's 25M capacity. Capacity does not need another upgrade yet.
+- Cancellations rose to 656, mostly S2 A/C/B. S2 issue age is 1.4 days and social still mentions support delays and output-quality regressions.
+- Week 26 adjustment: start tier-7 R&D, keep ads/capacity/prices stable, increase support/dev again, and post that the Day 182 tax fix is live with credits/validation for affected customers.
